patterns: when is the last date for admission
responses: The admission deadline for Fall 2026 is August 15, 2026. For Spring 2027, the deadline is January 10, 2027. We recommend applying early as seats fill up quickly!

patterns: what do I need to apply
responses: To apply, you need: 1) Minimum 60% in Intermediate/FSc or equivalent, 2) Valid CNIC/B-Form, 3) Recent passport-size photographs (4), 4) Original transcripts and certificates, 5) Clear the university entrance test, 6) Interview (for selected programs). International students additionally need IELTS/TOEFL scores.

Here's how to apply: Step 1: Visit the university admission portal and create an account. Step 2: Fill out the online application form with personal and academic details. Step 3: Upload required documents (CNIC, transcripts, photos). Step 4: Pay the application fee (Rs. 2,000) via bank challan or online. Step 5: Print your application form and fee challan. Step 6: Submit documents at the admissions office or mail them. Step 7: Appear for the entrance test on the given date. Step 8: Check results on the portal. Step 9: If selected, complete fee payment to confirm your seat!

Computer Science program covers: Semester 1: Programming Fundamentals, Calculus, English, ICT. Semester 2: Object-Oriented Programming, Discrete Math, Pakistan Studies, Physics. Semester 3: Data Structures, Digital Logic Design, Linear Algebra, Software Engineering. Semester 4: Database Systems, Computer Organization, Probability & Statistics, Technical Writing. Semester 5: Operating Systems, Computer Networks, Theory of Automata, Web Development. Semester 6: Artificial Intelligence, Software Project Management, Computer Architecture, Elective I. Semester 7: Information Security, Distributed Systems, Final Year Project I, Elective II. Semester 8: Professional Practices, Final Year Project II, Elective III, Elective IV.

The IT program includes: Programming Fundamentals, Web Technologies, Database Management, Network Administration, Cyber Security, Cloud Computing, Mobile App Development, Data Analytics, IT Project Management, System Administration, and Software Quality Assurance. Each semester has a mix of core and elective courses.

Here's the fee structure per semester: Admission Fee (One-time): Rs. 15,000. Tuition Fee: Rs. 85,000 per semester. Lab/Computer Fee: Rs. 10,000. Library Fee: Rs. 3,000. Examination Fee: Rs. 5,000. Student Activity Fee: Rs. 2,000. Total per semester: approximately Rs. 105,000. Hostel charges (if applicable): Rs. 25,000 per semester. Scholarships are available for merit and need-based students!

You can pay your fee through: 1) Online Student Portal — login and pay via credit/debit card or JazzCash/EasyPaisa. 2) Bank Transfer — deposit at HBL, MCB, or Allied Bank branches using the university challan. 3) University Cashier — pay directly at the finance office (Mon-Fri, 9 AM - 4 PM). 4) ATM/CDM — deposit via designated bank ATMs. Always keep your payment receipt! Fee deadline is the 10th of every month. Late fee: Rs. 500 per week.

We offer several scholarships: 1) Merit Scholarship — 100% tuition waiver for GPA 3.8+, 50% for GPA 3.5-3.79. 2) Need-Based Scholarship — up to 75% fee waiver based on financial need (requires income certificate). 3) HEC Scholarships — apply through the Higher Education Commission portal. 4) Alumni Scholarship — for children of university alumni (10% discount). 5) Sports Scholarship — for outstanding athletes (25-50% waiver). 6) Special Talent Scholarship — for achievements in arts, coding competitions, etc. Application: Submit the scholarship form with supporting documents by the 15th of each semester.

The examination schedule is as follows: Mid-Term Exams: 5th-15th of the 8th week of classes. Final-Term Exams: 2nd-20th of the 16th week. Supplementary/Improvement Exams: 2 weeks after result announcement. The detailed date sheet is published 2 weeks before exams on the student portal and notice boards. Practical exams are scheduled separately by each department. Make sure to collect your admit card before exams!

You can check your results by: 1) Logging into the Student Portal at portal.university.edu.pk and navigating to 'Academics > Results'. 2) Mid-term results are usually announced within 2 weeks. 3) Final-term results are announced within 4-6 weeks after exams end. 4) You can also visit your department office for a printed result card. 5) For any discrepancies, contact the Examination Department within 7 days of result announcement. GPA and transcripts are also available on the portal.

The University Library is open: Monday-Friday: 8:00 AM - 10:00 PM. Saturday: 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM. Sunday: 12:00 PM - 6:00 PM. Facilities include: 50,000+ physical books, Digital Library with IEEE, ACM, Springer access, 200+ study carrels, Group study rooms (book at the circulation desk), Computer lab with 50 workstations, Printing and photocopying services, 24/7 online catalog access. You can borrow up to 5 books for 14 days. Late fine: Rs. 10 per day per book. Digital resources are accessible via the library portal using your student ID.

University Hostel Facilities: Separate hostels for male and female students. Room types: 2-sharing (Rs. 25,000/sem), 3-sharing (Rs. 18,000/sem), 4-sharing (Rs. 15,000/sem). Facilities: Attached bathrooms, Wi-Fi, Common room with TV, Laundry service, Mess/Dining hall (3 meals a day), 24/7 security, Gym, Study rooms. Apply before semester starts — seats are limited! Application forms are available at the hostel office and student affairs department.

University Transport Service: Routes covering all major areas of the city. Pick-up points: 15 designated stops across the city. Monthly fee: Rs. 3,500. Semester pass: Rs. 15,000 (saves Rs. 2,000!). Timings: Morning pickups start 7:00 AM, Return buses depart 5:00 PM (Mon-Sat). GPS tracking available via the UniTransport app. To register: Fill the transport form at the Student Affairs office and pay the fee. New route requests can be submitted if 15+ students are from the same area.

Computer Labs: 8 state-of-the-art labs with 500+ systems. Lab 1-2: Programming Labs (C, C++, Java, Python). Lab 3-4: Database & Networking Labs (Oracle, MySQL, Cisco simulators). Lab 5-6: AI & Data Science Labs (TensorFlow, Jupyter, R Studio). Lab 7-8: Software Engineering & Project Labs. All labs have: Core i7 processors, 16GB RAM, SSD storage, High-speed internet, Projectors, Air conditioning. Lab hours: 8:00 AM - 8:00 PM (Mon-Sat). Students can use labs outside class hours by booking through the portal.

Upcoming Events: TechFest 2026 (Annual Technology Festival) — March 15-17, featuring coding competitions, hackathon, project exhibition, and guest lectures from industry experts. Cultural Night — April 5, celebrating diversity with performances and food stalls. Sports Week — April 20-25, including cricket, football, badminton, and e-sports tournaments. Career Fair — May 10, with 50+ companies recruiting. Seminars and workshops happen weekly — check the Events Board near the cafeteria or the university app for the full calendar!
